# Vernal Medicare

> Vernal Medicare is a local, licensed Medicare insurance agency in Vernal, Utah serving the Uintah Basin.
> Specializes in Medicare Advantage, Medigap (Medicare Supplement), Part D prescription drug plans, and under-65 health insurance.
> Agent: Rocco DeLuca. Phone: 435-219-5120. Address: 1080 W Hwy 40, Vernal, UT 84078 (inside Smith's Pharmacy).
> Last Updated: April 2026

## Complete Medicare Guide (Pillar Page)

- [/medicare-plans-vernal-utah.html]: **Medicare Plans in Vernal, Utah 2026 — The Complete Guide.** Comprehensive 3,200-word pillar covering all Medicare options for Vernal and Uintah Basin residents: Original Medicare (Parts A and B), Medicare Advantage (HMO/PPO), Medicare Supplement (Medigap Plans G and N), and Part D prescription drug plans. Includes 2026 costs, all enrollment periods (IEP, AEP, OEP, SEP), local hospitals (Ashley Regional, Uintah Basin Medical Center), pharmacies, and common mistakes to avoid. **This is the master guide that links to every other Medicare topic page on the site.**

## Medicare Education Guides

- [/medicare-part-a-vs-part-b.html]: Medicare Part A vs Part B Explained - Part A covers hospital stays, skilled nursing, and hospice (usually premium-free). Part B covers doctor visits, outpatient care, and preventive services ($185/month standard premium in 2026). Together they form Original Medicare.
- [/medicare-supplement-vs-advantage.html]: Medicare Supplement vs Medicare Advantage - Side-by-side comparison. Medigap works with Original Medicare for predictable costs and nationwide provider access. Advantage replaces Original Medicare with bundled coverage but uses provider networks. Important: switching from Advantage back to Medigap requires medical underwriting in Utah.
- [/when-to-enroll-medicare-utah.html]: When to Enroll in Medicare in Utah - Initial Enrollment Period (7 months around turning 65), Annual Enrollment (Oct 15–Dec 7), Open Enrollment (Jan 1–Mar 31), and Special Enrollment Periods. Missing deadlines results in permanent premium penalties.

- [/medicare-duchesne-utah.html]: Medicare Agent in Duchesne, Utah — Licensed agent serving Duchesne, Myton, Tabiona, and Duchesne County with free Medicare help.

## Enrollment & Deadlines

- [/medicare-open-enrollment-2026.html]: Medicare Open Enrollment 2026 — AEP dates (Oct 15–Dec 7), OEP (Jan 1–Mar 31), IEP, SEP rules and deadlines.

- [/missed-medicare-enrollment.html]: What Happens If I Miss Medicare Enrollment? — Part B penalty (10%/year permanent), Part D penalty, SEP options.
- [/medicare-checklist-2026.html]: 2026 Medicare Checklist Vernal Guide — Pre-enrollment checklist, key numbers, action steps for Uintah Basin residents.

- [/best-part-d-plans-vernal.html]: Best Medicare Part D Plans Vernal Utah 2026 — Formulary comparison, pharmacy pricing, $2,000 out-of-pocket cap, insulin $35 cap.
- [/cheapest-prescription-drug-plans.html]: Cheapest Prescription Drug Plans Vernal UT — Why lowest premium ≠ cheapest plan, Extra Help eligibility, pharmacy cost comparison.
- [/part-d-help-vernal.html]: Part D Help in Vernal — Local prescription drug plan guidance.

- [/does-medicare-cover-ozempic-wegovy.html]: Does Medicare Cover Ozempic or Wegovy 2026 — Ozempic covered for diabetes, Wegovy weight loss coverage limited.
- [/insulin-cost-medicare-vernal.html]: Cost of Insulin with Medicare Vernal Utah 2026 — $35/month cap, no deductible, applies at all pharmacies.
- [/tier-3-vs-tier-4-medicare-part-d.html]: Tier 3 vs Tier 4 Drugs on Medicare Part D Explained — 5-tier structure, why same drug varies by plan, cost reduction tips.

## Costs & Coverage Questions

- [/medicare-cost-uintah-county.html]: How Much Does Medicare Cost in Uintah County 2026 — Full cost breakdown: Part A/B, Advantage, Medigap, Part D, IRMAA.
- [/medicare-out-of-pocket-maximum-2026.html]: Medicare Out-of-Pocket Maximum 2026 — MOOP limits ($4,000–$9,350), Part D $2,000 cap, Original Medicare has no cap.
- [/does-medicare-cover-dental-vernal.html]: Does Medicare Cover Dental in Vernal Utah? — Original Medicare gaps, Advantage dental benefits, standalone dental plan options.
- [/medicare-out-of-state-utah.html]: Can I Use My Medicare Plan Out of State from Utah? — Medigap works nationwide, HMO restricted, PPO partial coverage.
- [/medicare-home-health-utah.html]: Medicare Home Health Coverage Utah 2026 — Eligibility (homebound + skilled need), covered services, $0 copay for most services.

## Service Area

- Serving: Vernal, Naples, Maeser, Jensen, Roosevelt, Duchesne, Myton, Tabiona, Altamont, Neola, and all of Uintah and Duchesne Counties, Utah
- Available at Smith's Pharmacy, 1080 W Highway 40, Vernal, UT 84078
- Hours: Mon-Fri 1pm-5pm, Sat by appointment
- Also available by phone, online, or home visits

## Medicare Enrollment Periods

- Initial Enrollment Period (IEP): 3 months before, month of, and 3 months after turning 65
- Annual Enrollment Period (AEP): October 15 - December 7 each year
- Medicare Advantage Open Enrollment (OEP): January 1 - March 31
- Special Enrollment Periods (SEP): Available for qualifying life events

## Common Questions

### How much does Medicare help cost in Vernal?
There is no cost to you. Vernal Medicare is paid by insurance carriers, so you receive free local Medicare advice, plan comparisons, and enrollment support.

### What Medicare plans are available in Vernal, Utah?
Vernal and Uintah County residents have access to Medicare Advantage (HMO and PPO), Medicare Supplement (Medigap Plans G and N), and Part D prescription drug plans. See the complete guide at /medicare-plans-vernal-utah.html.

### Can Rocco help with prescriptions and pharmacy costs?
Yes. We compare Part D plans based on your specific medications and preferred pharmacies in Vernal, including Smith's, Walgreens, and local options.

### Do you help with Medicaid and Extra Help?
Yes. Vernal Medicare assists with Utah Medicaid applications and Extra Help (Low-Income Subsidy) for prescription drug costs.

### What is the difference between Medicare Part A and Part B?
Part A covers hospital inpatient stays, skilled nursing, and hospice — most people pay no premium. Part B covers doctor visits, outpatient care, and preventive services for $185/month in 2026. Both together form Original Medicare. See our full guide at /medicare-part-a-vs-part-b.html.

### Should I choose Medigap or Medicare Advantage?
Medigap pairs with Original Medicare for predictable costs and nationwide provider freedom. Medicare Advantage replaces Original Medicare with bundled coverage but uses networks. For rural Uintah Basin residents, Medigap often provides more flexibility. See our comparison at /medicare-supplement-vs-advantage.html.

### When do I need to enroll in Medicare?
Your Initial Enrollment Period starts 3 months before you turn 65. Missing it can result in permanent premium penalties. See enrollment deadlines at /when-to-enroll-medicare-utah.html.

### Does Medicare cover Mounjaro or Ozempic?
Part D covers Ozempic and Mounjaro for type 2 diabetes. Weight loss coverage is evolving. Insulin is capped at $35/month. Total drug out-of-pocket is capped at $2,000/year in 2026. See /does-medicare-cover-mounjaro-utah.html and /does-medicare-cover-ozempic-wegovy.html.

### Which hospitals accept Medicare in Uintah County?
Ashley Regional Medical Center (Vernal) and Uintah Basin Medical Center (Roosevelt) both accept Original Medicare. Advantage plan coverage depends on your network. See /medicare-hospitals-uintah-county.html.

## Local Healthcare Providers

Works with clients who use:
- Ashley Regional Medical Center (Vernal) — see /medicare-ashley-regional-vernal.html
- Uintah Basin Medical Center (Roosevelt) — see /medicare-uintah-basin-medical-center.html
- Local Vernal clinics and specialists
